title: Idioms — Essential Everyday
description: Twenty idioms that appear in real English conversations all the time.
summary_pt_br: Idiomas essenciais: decorar como blocos, nunca traduzir palavra por palavra.

scenes.subtitle_en: Twenty idioms that appear in real English conversations all the time.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Idiomas essenciais: decorar como blocos, nunca traduzir palavra por palavra.

scenes.text_en: Twenty idioms that appear in real English conversations all the time. Today's focus: idioms — essential everyday. Let's call it a day; we've worked enough.
scenes.text_pt: Idiomas essenciais: decorar como blocos, nunca traduzir palavra por palavra. Foco de hoje: idioms — essential everyday. Vamos parar por hoje; já trabalhamos bastante.

scenes.title: Idioms — Essential Everyday
scenes.body_en: Memorize as whole phrases, never translate word-for-word. Examples: 'break the ice', 'hit the nail on the head', 'under the weather', 'call it a day', 'the ball is in your court'.
scenes.body_pt: Idiomas essenciais: decorar como blocos, nunca traduzir palavra por palavra.

examples.en: Let's call it a day; we've worked enough.
examples.pt: Vamos parar por hoje; já trabalhamos bastante.

examples.en: She hit the nail on the head in her analysis.
examples.pt: Ela acertou em cheio na análise.

examples.en: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.

items.term: hit the nail on the head

exercise.question: Complete: "Let's call it a day; we've ___ enough."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Let's call it a day; we've worked enough."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Vamos parar por hoje; já trabalhamos bastante.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
She hit the nail on the head in her analysis.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "She hit the nail on the head in her analysis."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Ela acertou em cheio na análise.

pairs: hit the nail on the head
exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Idioms — Essential Everyday — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre idioms — essential everyday. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You look tired today. Are you okay?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I'm feeling a bit under the weather.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Should we cut the meeting short?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, let's call it a day.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How was the presentation yesterday, actually?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: She hit the nail on the head with every slide.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Did you open with a joke to warm the room?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, just to break the ice.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What's next in our negotiation with the vendor?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The ball is in their court now.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Five idioms, one conversation. Fluency in disguise.

scenes.title: Idioms in the wild
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize the conversation using at least three idioms from the lesson.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: At the food truck's weekly planning, the team talked in a mix of plans, jokes, and well-worn idioms.

paragraphs.speaker: Rafa
paragraphs.en: Let's break the ice with the new supplier before we start negotiating price.

paragraphs.speaker: Employee
paragraphs.en: I'm under the weather today, so I may leave a little earlier than usual.

paragraphs.speaker: Rafa
paragraphs.en: No problem. We can call it a day once the lunch rush is done.

paragraphs.speaker: Employee
paragraphs.en: The idea about the vegan menu hit the nail on the head — it's what customers want.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Idioms in English, Rafa liked to say, are the glue that makes sentences sound alive.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Idioms — Essential Everyday". Twenty idioms that appear in real English conversations all the time. Let's call it a day; we've worked enough. She hit the nail on the head in her analysis. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Idioms — Essential Everyday". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Idioms — Essential Everyday".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
